title: Sleep Episode
description: This schema represents one sleep episode.

latency_to_sleep_onset.description: Amount of time between when person starts to want to go to sleep and sleep onset.

latency_to_arising.description: Amount of time between final awakening and when person stops wanting to go to sleep.

total_sleep_time.description: The total amount of time spent asleep within the effective time frame.

light_sleep_duration.description: The amount of total sleep time spent in light sleep.

deep_sleep_duration.description: The amount of total sleep time spent in deep sleep.

rem_sleep_duration.description: The amount of total sleep time spent in REM sleep.

wake_after_sleep_onset.description: The total duration of all awakenings between sleep onset and final awakening.

is_main_sleep.description: Whether the sleep episode is the main sleep event (i.e., a night sleep for most people) or a nap.

effective_time_frame.description: The time interval during which the measurement is asserted as being valid. As a measure of a duration, total sleep time should not be associated to a date time time frame. Hence, effective time frame is restricted to be a time interval. The initial sleep onset time maps to start_date_time, the final awakening time maps to end_date_time and total sleep episode duration maps to duration.

sleep_efficiency_percentage.description: The amount of time spent asleep as a percentage of the sleep episode bounded by the effective time frame.
